title: The Chain Rule Intuition
concept: The chain rule says an early weight affects the final loss through every step after it, so backpropagation follows the full cause-and-effect chain instead of looking at one step alone.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why does backpropagation move backward through all the layers instead of checking only the output layer?

options.text: Because earlier weights can still influence the final loss through later layers.
Because hidden layers never use weights.
Because the loss node cannot connect to the output layer.

exercises.explain: Correct! Even a weight far from the output can shape the final error through the chain of later operations.

cards.title: One faucet, many pipes
cards.body: You turn a faucet handle by a tiny amount. The water pressure at the showerhead changes, but only after the twist travels through valves, pipes, and the nozzle. In a neural network, an early weight also affects the final loss through a whole chain of later steps.

cards.title: What the chain rule means here
cards.body: In backpropagation, the chain rule means we measure an early weight by following every link between that weight and the final loss. Each operation passes along part of the effect, so the full answer comes from the whole chain, not one isolated step.

cards.title: A train of gears
cards.body: Imagine a row of gears. Turning the first gear changes the second, which changes the third, which changes the fourth. To know how the first gear affects the last one, you must consider every gear in between. That is chain-rule thinking.

cards.title: From knob to speaker volume
cards.body: A music app has a volume knob feeding into an amplifier, then into a speaker. If the song ends up too loud, you can ask: how much did a tiny twist of the knob affect the final loudness? The answer depends on the knob, the amplifier, and the speaker together — the full chain.

cards.title: It is not random blame sharing
cards.body: Backpropagation is not guessing which layer to blame. The chain rule gives a structured path for the feedback to travel, so each layer gets correction information based on how it connects to the final loss.

cards.title: Single step vs. full chain
cards.body: How an early change reaches the loss:

left.text:
Asks what happens at one nearby operation.
Useful but incomplete alone.

right.text:
Follows every link after the weight.
Needed to know the final effect on loss.

exercises.prompt: Which idea best matches the chain rule in backpropagation?

options.text: An early weight affects the loss through a sequence of later operations, so the whole path matters.
Only the final layer matters because hidden layers are skipped during learning.
The model chooses a random layer to update each time.
Loss can only depend on the dataset size.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The chain rule is about connected cause-and-effect across multiple steps.

exercises.prompt: True or false: If a weight sits in an early hidden layer, its effect on loss may pass through several later operations before reaching the output.
exercises.explain: True! That long path is exactly why the chain rule is needed.

exercises.sentence: The chain rule helps backpropagation follow a full cause-and-effect ___ from an early weight to the loss.
exercises.explain: Correct. The model tracks how influence moves through the connected chain of operations.

exercises.prompt: Which situation is the best analogy for the chain rule?

options.text: A small turn of the first gear changes every later gear until the last gear moves.
A student flips directly from question 1 to the final grade with no steps in between.
A camera stores a photo without altering it.

exercises.explain: Correct! The gear train captures the idea that effects pass through linked steps.

exercises.prompt: A hidden-layer weight changes the output only after its value passes through another hidden layer and then the output layer. To know how that weight affects the final loss, what must backpropagation do?

options.text: Follow the full chain of later operations from that weight to the loss.
Ignore the hidden layers and inspect only the loss number itself.
Retrain the model from scratch without a backward pass.
Assume every weight changes the loss by the same amount.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The chain rule lets the network connect an early weight to the final error through every intermediate step. Next, we will zoom in on what each individual step contributes: its local slope.
